rule CDB_ISC_Warns_of_HighSeverity_Kea_DHCP_Flaw_ {
    meta:
        author = "CyberDudeBivash GOC - Sentinel APEX v39.0"
        description = "Detection for ISC Warns of High-Severity Kea DHCP Flaw That Can Crash Services Remotely"
        severity = "high"
        date = "2026-03-27"
    strings:
                $actor = "UNC-UNKNOWN" ascii wide nocase
    condition:
        any of them
}

executive_briefing.executive_summary: During the reporting period, the CYBERDUDEBIVASH Sentinel APEX platform processed 500 threat advisories. Of these, 104 are classified as CRITICAL risk and 77 as HIGH risk. 36 advisories involve vulnerabilities confirmed as actively exploited (CISA KEV). Primary threat actors include UNC-CDB-99, UNC-UNKNOWN, CDB-APT-22. The overall threat posture requires IMMEDIATE executive attention with focus on patch management and detection rule deployment.
